title: Cacalia compactiflora
intro: Cacalia compactiflora, also known as Asian buttonbush or small-flowered buttonbush, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditional medicine has utilized this plant for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia compactiflora include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cacalia compactiflora is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Cacalia compactiflora belongs to the family Asteraceae and is classified as a species within the genus Cacalia. It is a flowering plant native to East Asia. The classification is also reflected in its taxonomic rank, with Cacalia compactiflora being a species within the family level and the genus level. Further classification involves its order, family, and class, all of which contribute to its complete taxonomic hierarchy. Understanding the classification of Cacalia compactiflora is essential in its identification and conservation.
